mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of rising rate instances.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances or the t-statistic for the signal falls below 1.5 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP growth and rising personal savings rates indicates robust household balance sheets funding consumption, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through sustained earnings growth.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative returns in > 50% of cases or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative returns in > 55% of instances during early-cycle expansions or rolling IC drops below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 330.0, elevated inflation expectations force markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day returns are positive in > 65% of threshold-crossing events or predictive accuracy falls below 52% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict positive returns in > 50% of observations or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher monthly housing starts signal strong residential investment demand and construction sector activity, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through multiplier effects on related industries.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window or housing starts show no significant correlation with 60-day equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the index of industrial production falls below the 2017 baseline of 100.0, it indicates a contraction in manufacturing output that triggers immediate sell-offs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power during contractionary periods.
